message:
chore(lints): rewrite last `if let` in duration-suffix parse as `?`

`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` flagged the trailing
`else if let Some(stripped) = s.strip_suffix('w') { ... } else { return
None; };` in the duration-suffix parser as rewritable with the `?`
operator. The earlier h/d/m branches keep their `if let` form because
they don't terminate on failure — they fall through to the next suffix
check — so only the final branch is eligible for the lint.

Semantics are identical: if `s` ends with 'w', return the stripped
prefix paired with the "w" unit tag; if it ends with none of h/d/m/w,
return None from the outer function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
